moduleDescription:
Arbitrary precision integers.
  • https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/BigInt

entries.name: JsBigInt
entries.code: JsBigInt = distinct JsBigIntImpl
entries.description: Arbitrary precision integer for JavaScript target.

entries.name: big
entries.code:
func big(integer: SomeInteger): JsBigInt {.importjs: "BigInt(#)", raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}

entries.name: `'big`
entries.code:
func `'big`(num: cstring): JsBigInt {.importjs: "BigInt(#)", raises: [],
                                      tags: [], forbids: [].}

entries.name: big
entries.code:
func big(integer: cstring): JsBigInt {.importjs: "BigInt(#)", raises: [],
                                       tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Alias for 'big

entries.name: toCstring
entries.code:
func toCstring(this: JsBigInt; radix: 2 .. 36): cstring {.
    importjs: "#.toString(#)", raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Converts from JsBigInt to cstring representation.
  • radix Base to use for representing numeric values.

https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/BigInt/toString

entries.name: toCstring
entries.code:
func toCstring(this: JsBigInt): cstring {.importjs: "#.toString()", raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Converts from JsBigInt to cstring representation. https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/BigInt/toString

entries.code: func `$`(this: JsBigInt): string {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns a string representation of JsBigInt.

entries.name: wrapToInt

pragmas: importjs: "(() => { const i = #, b = #; return BigInt.asIntN(b, i) })()"

entries.description: Wraps this to a signed JsBigInt of bits bits in -2 ^ (bits - 1) .. 2 ^ (bits - 1) - 1. https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/BigInt/asIntN

entries.name: wrapToUint

pragmas: importjs: "(() => { const i = #, b = #; return BigInt.asUintN(b, i) })()"

entries.description: Wraps this to an unsigned JsBigInt of bits bits in 0 .. 2 ^ bits - 1. https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/BigInt/asUintN

entries.name: toNumber
entries.code:
func toNumber(this: JsBigInt): int {.importjs: "Number(#)", raises: [],
                                     tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Does not do any bounds check and may or may not return an inexact representation.

entries.code:
func `+`(x, y: JsBigInt): JsBigInt {.importjs: "(# $1 #)", raises: [], tags: [],
                                     forbids: [].}

func `-`(x, y: JsBigInt): JsBigInt {.importjs: "(# $1 #)", raises: [], tags: [],
                                     forbids: [].}

func `*`(x, y: JsBigInt): JsBigInt {.importjs: "(# $1 #)", raises: [], tags: [],
                                     forbids: [].}

entries.name: `div`
entries.code:
func `div`(x, y: JsBigInt): JsBigInt {.importjs: "(# / #)", raises: [],
                                       tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Same as div but for JsBigInt(uses JavaScript BigInt() / BigInt()).

entries.name: `mod`
entries.code:
func `mod`(x, y: JsBigInt): JsBigInt {.importjs: "(# % #)", raises: [],
                                       tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Same as mod but for JsBigInt (uses JavaScript BigInt() % BigInt()).

entries.name: inc
entries.code:
func inc(this: var JsBigInt) {.importjs: "(++[#][0][0])", raises: [], tags: [],
                               forbids: [].}

entries.name: dec
entries.code:
func dec(this: var JsBigInt) {.importjs: "(--[#][0][0])", raises: [], tags: [],
                               forbids: [].}

entries.name: inc
entries.code:
func inc(this: var JsBigInt; amount: JsBigInt) {.importjs: "([#][0][0] += #)",
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

entries.name: dec
entries.code:
func dec(this: var JsBigInt; amount: JsBigInt) {.importjs: "([#][0][0] -= #)",
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

func `+=`(x: var JsBigInt; y: JsBigInt) {.importjs: "([#][0][0] $1 #)",
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

func `-=`(x: var JsBigInt; y: JsBigInt) {.importjs: "([#][0][0] $1 #)",
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

func `*=`(x: var JsBigInt; y: JsBigInt) {.importjs: "([#][0][0] $1 #)",
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

func `/=`(x: var JsBigInt; y: JsBigInt) {.importjs: "([#][0][0] $1 #)",
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Same as x = x div y.

entries.code: proc `+`(_: JsBigInt): JsBigInt {.error: "See https://github.com/tc39/proposal-bigint/blob/master/ADVANCED.md#dont-break-asmjs".}

entries.name: low
entries.code:
proc low(_: typedesc[JsBigInt]): JsBigInt {.
    error: "Arbitrary precision integers do not have a known low.".}

pragmas: error: "Arbitrary precision integers do not have a known low."

entries.name: high
entries.code:
proc high(_: typedesc[JsBigInt]): JsBigInt {.
    error: "Arbitrary precision integers do not have a known high.".}

pragmas: error: "Arbitrary precision integers do not have a known high."
